Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Binary Endpoints

Source: R/getgamma2bin.R
getgamma2bin.Rd

Computes the optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_1\) and NoGo threshold \(\gamma_2\) for two binary endpoints by searching over a two-dimensional grid of candidate value pairs. The search follows a two-stage precompute-then-sweep strategy: region probabilities \(\hat{g}_{Go,ij}\) and \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij}\) are precomputed once for every possible multinomial outcome combination \((x_t, x_c)\), and operating characteristics under each \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) pair are obtained by a fast weighted summation without additional Dirichlet draws.

Usage

getgamma2bin(
  prob = "posterior",
  design = "controlled",
  GoRegions,
  NoGoRegions,
  pi_t1,
  pi_t2,
  rho_t,
  pi_c1 = NULL,
  pi_c2 = NULL,
  rho_c = NULL,
  target_go,
  target_nogo,
  crit_go = "<",
  crit_nogo = "<",
  sel_go = "smallest",
  sel_nogo = "largest",
  n1,
  n2,
  a1_00,
  a1_01,
  a1_10,
  a1_11,
  a2_00,
  a2_01,
  a2_10,
  a2_11,
  theta.TV1 = NULL,
  theta.MAV1 = NULL,
  theta.TV2 = NULL,
  theta.MAV2 = NULL,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL,
  theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL,
  m2 = NULL,
  z00 = NULL,
  z01 = NULL,
  z10 = NULL,
  z11 = NULL,
  xe1_00 = NULL,
  xe1_01 = NULL,
  xe1_10 = NULL,
  xe1_11 = NULL,
  xe2_00 = NULL,
  xe2_01 = NULL,
  xe2_10 = NULL,
  xe2_11 = NULL,
  ae1 = NULL,
  ae2 = NULL,
  nMC = 1000L,
  method = "Exact",
  nsim = 10000L,
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01)
)

Arguments

prob

A character string specifying the probability type. Must be 'posterior' or 'predictive'.

design

A character string specifying the trial design. Must be 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', or 'external'.

GoRegions

An integer vector of region indices (subset of 1:9) that constitute the Go region. The 9 regions are defined by the cross-classification of the two treatment effects \((\theta_1, \theta_2)\) relative to (TV1, MAV1) and (TV2, MAV2): region \(k = (z_1 - 1) \times 3 + z_2\) where \(z_i = 1\) if \(\theta_i > TV_i\), \(z_i = 2\) if \(MAV_i < \theta_i \le TV_i\), \(z_i = 3\) if \(\theta_i \le MAV_i\).

NoGoRegions

An integer vector of region indices (subset of 1:9) that constitute the NoGo region. Must be disjoint from GoRegions.

pi_t1

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the true treatment response probability for Endpoint 1 for the scenario used to search for the optimal thresholds.

pi_t2

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the true treatment response probability for Endpoint 2.

rho_t

A numeric scalar giving the within-arm correlation in the treatment arm.

pi_c1

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the true control response probability for Endpoint 1. Required for design = 'controlled' or 'external'; set to NULL for design = 'uncontrolled'.

pi_c2

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the true control response probability for Endpoint 2. Required for design = 'controlled' or 'external'; set to NULL for design = 'uncontrolled'.

rho_c

A numeric scalar giving the within-arm correlation in the control arm. Required for design = 'controlled' or 'external'; set to NULL for design = 'uncontrolled'.

target_go

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the target value for \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) used to select the optimal \(\gamma_1\). For each candidate \(\gamma_1\), the worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) over all \(\gamma_2\) in gamma2_grid is compared against target_go using the operator specified by crit_go.

target_nogo

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the target value for \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) used to select the optimal \(\gamma_2\). For each candidate \(\gamma_2\), the worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) over all \(\gamma_1\) in gamma1_grid is compared against target_nogo using the operator specified by crit_nogo.

crit_go

A character string specifying the comparison operator applied to \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) when searching for \(\gamma_1\). Must be one of "<", "<=", ">", or ">=". Default is "<".

crit_nogo

A character string specifying the comparison operator applied to \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) when searching for \(\gamma_2\). Must be one of "<", "<=", ">", or ">=". Default is "<".

sel_go

A character string specifying whether to select the "smallest" or "largest" value in gamma1_grid satisfying the crit_go criterion. Default is "smallest".

sel_nogo

A character string specifying whether to select the "smallest" or "largest" value in gamma2_grid satisfying the crit_nogo criterion. Default is "largest".

n1

A positive integer giving the number of patients in group 1 (treatment arm) in the PoC trial.

n2

A positive integer giving the number of patients in group 2 (control arm) in the PoC trial.

a1_00

A positive numeric scalar giving the Dirichlet prior parameter for the (0,0) response pattern in group 1.

a1_01

A positive numeric scalar; see a1_00.

a1_10

A positive numeric scalar; see a1_00.

a1_11

A positive numeric scalar; see a1_00.

a2_00

A positive numeric scalar giving the Dirichlet prior parameter for the (0,0) response pattern in group 2.

a2_01

A positive numeric scalar; see a2_00.

a2_10

A positive numeric scalar; see a2_00.

a2_11

A positive numeric scalar; see a2_00.

theta.TV1

A numeric scalar giving the TV threshold for Endpoint 1. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.MAV1

A numeric scalar giving the MAV threshold for Endpoint 1. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.TV2

A numeric scalar giving the TV threshold for Endpoint 2. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.MAV2

A numeric scalar giving the MAV threshold for Endpoint 2. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.NULL1

A numeric scalar giving the null hypothesis threshold for Endpoint 1. Required when prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.NULL2

A numeric scalar giving the null hypothesis threshold for Endpoint 2. Required when prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set to NULL.

m1

A positive integer giving the future sample size for group 1. Required when prob = 'predictive'; set to NULL otherwise.

m2

A positive integer giving the future sample size for group 2. Required when prob = 'predictive'; set to NULL otherwise.

z00

A non-negative integer giving the hypothetical control count for pattern (0,0). Required when design = 'uncontrolled'; otherwise set to NULL.

z01

A non-negative integer; see z00.

z10

A non-negative integer; see z00.

z11

A non-negative integer; see z00.

xe1_00

A non-negative integer giving the external group 1 count for pattern (0,0). Required when design = 'external' and external treatment data are used; otherwise NULL.

xe1_01

A non-negative integer; see xe1_00.

xe1_10

A non-negative integer; see xe1_00.

xe1_11

A non-negative integer; see xe1_00.

xe2_00

A non-negative integer giving the external group 2 count for pattern (0,0). Required when design = 'external' and external control data are used; otherwise NULL.

xe2_01

A non-negative integer; see xe2_00.

xe2_10

A non-negative integer; see xe2_00.

xe2_11

A non-negative integer; see xe2_00.

ae1

A numeric scalar in (0, 1] giving the power prior weight for external group 1 data. Required when external treatment data are used; otherwise NULL.

ae2

A numeric scalar in (0, 1] giving the power prior weight for external group 2 data. Required when external control data are used; otherwise NULL.

nMC

A positive integer giving the number of Dirichlet draws used to evaluate region probabilities for each count combination in Stage 1. Default is 1000L.

method

A character string specifying the computation method passed to pbayespostpred2bin internally. Must be 'Exact' (default) or 'MC'.

nsim

A positive integer giving the number of outer Monte Carlo samples used when method = 'MC'. Default is 10000L.

gamma1_grid

A numeric vector of candidate Go threshold values in (0, 1) to search over. Defaults to seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01).

gamma2_grid

A numeric vector of candidate NoGo threshold values in (0, 1) to search over. Defaults to seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01).

Value

A list of class getgamma2bin with the following elements:

gamma1

Optimal Go threshold selected from gamma1_grid according to crit_go and sel_go. NA if no value satisfies the criterion.

gamma2

Optimal NoGo threshold selected from gamma2_grid according to crit_nogo and sel_nogo. NA if no value satisfies the criterion.

PrGo_at_gamma

Pr(Go) evaluated at (gamma1, gamma2). NA if either threshold is NA.

PrNoGo_at_gamma

Pr(NoGo) evaluated at (gamma1, gamma2). NA if either threshold is NA.

gamma1_grid

The candidate Go threshold grid used.

gamma2_grid

The candidate NoGo threshold grid used.

PrGo_grid

Numeric matrix of dimensions length(gamma1_grid) x length(gamma2_grid) giving Pr(Go) at each grid point.

PrNoGo_grid

Numeric matrix of the same dimensions giving Pr(NoGo) at each grid point.

Details

The function uses the same two-stage precompute-then-sweep strategy as pbayesdecisionprob2bin.

Stage 1 (precomputation): pbayespostpred2bin is called for every possible multinomial outcome combination \((x_t, x_c)\) enumerated by allmultinom. The resulting 9-element region probability vector is summed over GoRegions and NoGoRegions to obtain \(\hat{g}_{Go,ij}\) and \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij}\), the Go/NoGo probabilities for each combination, independent of the decision thresholds \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\).

Stage 2 (gamma sweep): For each pair \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) in the two-dimensional grid, the operating characteristics are computed as: $$\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \sum_{i,j} w_{ij} \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{Go,ij} \ge \gamma_1,\; \hat{g}_{NoGo,ij} < \gamma_2\right]$$ $$\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \sum_{i,j} w_{ij} \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{NoGo,ij} \ge \gamma_2,\; \hat{g}_{Go,ij} < \gamma_1\right]$$ where \(w_{ij} = P(X_t = x_{t,i}) \times P(X_c = x_{c,j})\) are the multinomial probability weights. No additional Dirichlet draws are required for this step.

Stage 3 (optimal threshold selection): For each candidate \(\gamma_1\), the maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) over all \(\gamma_2\) in gamma2_grid is computed; the optimal \(\gamma_1\) is then chosen as the sel_go value satisfying the crit_go comparison against target_go. Analogously, the optimal \(\gamma_2\) is the sel_nogo value for which the maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) over all \(\gamma_1\) satisfies crit_nogo against target_nogo.

Examples

# Example 1: Controlled design, posterior probability
# gamma1: smallest gamma1 s.t. max_{gamma2} Pr(Go)   < 0.05
# gamma2: largest  gamma2 s.t. max_{gamma1} Pr(NoGo) < 0.20
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
getgamma2bin(
  prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.20, rho_t = 0.0,
  pi_c1 = 0.15, pi_c2 = 0.20, rho_c = 0.0,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,
  a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,
  a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,
  theta.TV1   = 0.15, theta.MAV1 = 0.10,
  theta.TV2   = 0.15, theta.MAV2 = 0.10,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,
  xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,
  xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,
  ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,
  nMC = 100L, method = 'Exact',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01)
)
} # }

# Example 2: Uncontrolled design, posterior probability
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
getgamma2bin(
  prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.20, rho_t = 0.0,
  pi_c1 = NULL, pi_c2 = NULL, rho_c = NULL,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,
  a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,
  a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,
  theta.TV1   = 0.15, theta.MAV1 = 0.10,
  theta.TV2   = 0.15, theta.MAV2 = 0.10,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  z00 = 3L, z01 = 2L, z10 = 3L, z11 = 2L,
  xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,
  xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,
  ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,
  nMC = 100L, method = 'Exact',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01)
)
} # }

# Example 3: Controlled design, predictive probability
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
getgamma2bin(
  prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.20, rho_t = 0.0,
  pi_c1 = 0.15, pi_c2 = 0.20, rho_c = 0.0,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,
  a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,
  a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,
  theta.TV1   = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,
  theta.TV2   = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,
  theta.NULL1 = 0.10, theta.NULL2 = 0.10,
  m1 = 30L, m2 = 30L,
  z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,
  xe1_00 = NULL, xe1_01 = NULL, xe1_10 = NULL, xe1_11 = NULL,
  xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,
  ae1 = NULL, ae2 = NULL,
  nMC = 100L, method = 'Exact',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01)
)
} # }

# Example 4: External design, predictive probability
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
getgamma2bin(
  prob = 'predictive', design = 'external',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  pi_t1 = 0.15, pi_t2 = 0.20, rho_t = 0.0,
  pi_c1 = 0.15, pi_c2 = 0.20, rho_c = 0.0,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 10L, n2 = 10L,
  a1_00 = 0.25, a1_01 = 0.25, a1_10 = 0.25, a1_11 = 0.25,
  a2_00 = 0.25, a2_01 = 0.25, a2_10 = 0.25, a2_11 = 0.25,
  theta.TV1   = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,
  theta.TV2   = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,
  theta.NULL1 = 0.10, theta.NULL2 = 0.10,
  m1 = 30L, m2 = 30L,
  z00 = NULL, z01 = NULL, z10 = NULL, z11 = NULL,
  xe1_00 = 3L, xe1_01 = 2L, xe1_10 = 3L, xe1_11 = 2L,
  xe2_00 = NULL, xe2_01 = NULL, xe2_10 = NULL, xe2_11 = NULL,
  ae1 = 0.5, ae2 = NULL,
  nMC = 100L, method = 'Exact',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01)
)
} # }
